entries.name: CreateAssemblyMirroringCommand
entries.title: This command creates a mirror assembly. A mirror assembly is created from the composition of parts of another assembly on which other operations can be added. It will be symmetrical with respect to the original assembly (left part and right part).

entries.name: CreatePartMirroringCommand
entries.title: Mirror part
entries.summary: - If the geometry of the part used to make the mirror part changes, the geometry of the mirror part changes too.

entries.name: DeriveFromCommand
entries.title: This command (only available in a function document) allows to modify an independently created **Part function** document while it should be the derivative of another function (see [Derived function](CreatePartFunctionDerivationCommand.md)). So it is possible to force this existing function document to be the derivative of another function without creating again it from the function document which it must be derived.
entries.summary: This command (only available in a function document) allows to modify an independently created **Part function** document while it should be the derivative of another function (see [Derived function](CreatePartFunctionDerivationCommand.md)). So it is possible to force this existing function document to be the derivative of another function without creating again it from the function document which it must be derived.

entries.name: EditDerivationCommand

entries.summary: The derived part or assembly or the mirror part or assembly has to be opened.

entries.name: MirrorPartCommand
entries.title: This popup command allows you to make a mirror part from a part designed in place. A mirror part is created from the geometry of another part. The mirror part will be symmetrical with respect to this part (left part and right part). If the mirror part must be equal to the original part, the function [Symmetry Plane](../Symmetries/PlaneSymmetryCommand.md) or [Symmetry Axis](../Symmetries/RevolutionSymmetryCommand.md) must be used.
entries.summary: This popup command allows you to make a mirror part from a part designed in place. A mirror part is created from the geometry of another part. The mirror part will be symmetrical with respect to this part (left part and right part). If the mirror part must be equal to the original part, the function [Symmetry Plane](../Symmetries/PlaneSymmetryCommand.md) or [Symmetry Axis](../Symmetries/RevolutionSymmetryCommand.md) must be used.

entries.name: SmartClearanceHoleStandardParameterCommand
entries.title: This command creates a parameter based on a standardized or customized clearance hole standard. This parameter can be used when creating drillings. By modifying this parameter (by connecting it to another standard), all the drillings which use this parameter will be modified.

This referencing is done by using the **[Add reference](../../../../../Pdm/UI/Projects/ReferenceProjectCommand.md)** command from the contextual menu on the References icon in the Projects tree.

It also allows to define when these displacements must be executed. This animation can be [played](PlayAnimationCommand.md) and/or [recorded](RecordAnimationCommand.md).

entries.name: RenameMountingStagesCommand
entries.title: By default, the title of the first mounting stage created is numbered "1", which in fact corresponds to the case of descending: at the start everything is up, it's the start of the chronology of the explosion. When you would like to create an mounting notice, you must rename the stages to put them in reverse chronological order.

entries.name: CoatingRelayParameterCommand

If the [coating parameter](CoatingParameterCommand.md) has been created in an assembly, the coating relay parameter allows to apply it on each in place part. Then, you just have to change the coating of this parameter in the assembly to change it on each in place part. A coating relay parameter has to be created in each in place part and the [coating](../MaterialAndCoatingCommand.md) command for these parts has to be set to **Parameter.**

entries.name: MaterialRelayParameterCommand
entries.title: This parameter allows to relay the [material parameter](MaterialParameterCommand.md) of another document, component or project. If the [material parameter](MaterialParameterCommand.md) has been created in an assembly, the material relay parameter allows to apply it on each in place part. Then you just have to change this parameter in the assembly and this material will be applied on each in place part. A material relay parameter has to be created in each in place part and the [material](../MaterialAndCoatingCommand.md) command for these parts has to be set to **Parameter.**

The components (flanges, tees, reducers,...) could be added with the [Insert](PipingSketchInsertCommand.md) contextual command.

entries.name: CurrentRepresentationCommand
entries.title: This command selects the current representation displayed in the workspace
entries.summary: - To be able to select a representation, a [simplified representation](SimplifiedRepresentationCommand.md) or an [other representation](RepresentationCommand.md) needs to have been created on the part beforehand.

entries.name: DuplicateIntoRepresentationCommand
entries.title: Duplicate in Representation
entries.summary: Use this command to create the **Detailed representation** and **Simplified representation** sub-folders if there are none. The entities selected with this command will be duplicated in the sub-folder of the selected representation..

entries.name: RepresentationCommand
entries.title: This command allows you to create a new representation of the document
entries.summary: - A simplified representation keeps the information of the part in detailed representation in the BOM.

entries.name: SimplifiedRepresentationCommand
entries.title: This command creates a simplified representation. A simplified part is a part which has been stripped down visually to improve system performances. For example,on a full machine, there is no point representing 3000 screws with all details, (threading, etc.) a representation with an axis line is sufficient.
entries.summary: - The **Simplified representation** sub-folder cannot be renamed.

entries.name: CurrentRigidGroupCommand
entries.title: This command allows you to select the current configuration displayed in the workspace
entries.summary: - To be able to select a configuration, a [constrained configuration](../Configurations/Operations/ConstrainedConfigurationCommand.md) needs to have been created beforehand on the part.

entries.name: MergeRigidGroupsCommand
entries.title: This command merges rigid groups. The contents of the 2 rigid groups will be put into the rigid group to be conserved.

entries.name: RigidGroupCommand
entries.title: Rigid group
entries.summary: A "ghost" rigid group can be created. This is a rigid set that contains only one point or frame, allowing you to make an intermediate joint. For example. A is a rigid group that must be moved linearly and around an axis in relation to a rigid set B. F is a "ghost" rigid set. You can place a **prismatic** joint between A and F, and a **pivot**

entries.name: RigidGroupMomentCommand
entries.title: This command provides information on the moment of inertia of a rigid group.
entries.summary: This command provides information on the moment of inertia of a rigid group.

entries.name: SmartRigidGroupPublishingCommand
entries.title: SmartRigidGroupPublishingCommand

entries.name: inplaceschematiccommand
entries.title: In Place Schematic
entries.summary: - Schematics are created in the generic document of a component, or directly in a part or assembly document.

entries.name: schematicinclusioncommand
entries.title: Include Schematic
entries.summary: - The schematics created by the inclusion are "stored" in the Schematics folder of the entity tree.

entries.title: Assembly -> Part

entries.name: SelectionModeCommand
entries.title: These modes allow you to set the selection direction of the entities to the use of the [rotating selection](../../../../../../Common/Rotative_Picking_.md).

entries.name: SelectByPropertiesCommand
entries.title: This command allows to select parts by properties.
entries.summary: This command allows to select parts by properties.

entries.name: SetCommand
entries.title: The **Set** command allows to insert different sub-parts of the assembly in different sets.
entries.summary: - You can drag a set in a set from the entities tree.

entries.title: This command allows you to limit 2 shapes one in relation to the other, in an assembly.
entries.summary: This command allows you to limit 2 shapes one in relation to the other, in an assembly.

entries.summary: This command allows you to trim a part of an assembly by profile.

entries.title: This command allows you to trim a part in an assembly, by surface, face
entries.summary: This command allows you to trim a part in an assembly, by surface, face or plane.

entries.name: ReciprocalTrimCommand
entries.title: Reciprocal trimming - Part
entries.summary: This command allows you to limit 2 shapes one in relation to the other, in an assembly.

entries.summary: This command allows you to trim a part of an assembly by profile.

entries.title: This command allows you to trim a part in an assembly, by surface, face or plane.
entries.summary: This command allows you to trim a part in an assembly, by surface, face or plane.

entries.title: This command allows to divide a batch of parts by a section.
entries.summary: - The division transforms the modified parts into local parts.

entries.title: In Place Extruded Parts
entries.summary: This command allows to extrude several shapes from several planar sections defined in an assembly. Each shape becomes an in place part of the assembly.

entries.summary: - It is necessary to determine the extremity faces on all shapes of the profile in all its representations.

entries.summary: To be able to use the extruded bars, it is essential that the libraries required for the project be referenced beforehand. The referencing is done via the **[Add reference](../../../../../Pdm/UI/Projects/ReferenceProjectCommand.md)** contextual command from the references icon in the project tree.

entries.summary: This command allows you to create an extruded bar kit, consisting of an assembly document and its family.

entries.name: TrimCommand
entries.title: This command allows you to create a planar cross section on [extruded
entries.summary: This command allows you to create a planar cross section on [extruded bars](ExtrudedBarCommand.md). The first extruded bar will extend to the selected plane on the tool extruded bar.

entries.name: GrooveShoulderCommand
entries.title: Groove or shoulder
entries.summary: This command creates grooves or shoulders on cylindrical or tapered shapes. The geometry achieved is linked to the choice of a groove/shoulder template from the referenced library

entries.name: CheckCommand
entries.title: This command allows to check the geometry of the parts of an assembly
entries.summary: This command allows to check the geometry of the parts of an assembly in order to check if they are valid or not.

entries.name: CleanCommand
entries.title: This command allows to quickly repair some invalid items in invalid
entries.summary: This command allows to quickly repair some invalid items in invalid parts (generally following an import).

entries.name: SimplifyCommand
entries.title: This command simplifies the topology of a shape by converting surfaces
entries.summary: This command simplifies the topology of a shape by converting surfaces that can be it and the edges of online faces into a planar, cylindrical, or conical surface, or into arcs for edges that can be it, while complying with the given tolerance.

entries.name: KnurlingCommand
entries.title: This command allows you to create a strait or crossed knurling on a cylindrical face of a shape.
entries.summary: The created knurling is a single texture wrap. It does not physically modify the geometry of the part. As a result, knurling will only appear on shadowed views in the draft.

entries.name: MachiningProcessCommand
entries.title: Machining process
entries.summary: This command allows to assign a machining process (name of a process and a color) to several faces. These processes are then known by TopSolid'Cam.

entries.name: MarkingCommand
entries.title: This command allows to imprint a property,a
entries.summary: This command allows to imprint a property,a text or a sketch on a part face.

entries.name: PartsMarkingCommand
entries.title: Marking (Assembly)
entries.summary: This command allows to apply marking defined in the part document to the shape.

entries.name: editpatternmodifiercommand
entries.title: Edite pattern modifier
entries.summary: This command allows you to modify the pattern of a family directly from the assembly.

entries.name: PipeUnbendingCommand
entries.title: Pipe unbending
entries.summary: This command allows to unbend elbows of a shape.

entries.name: PartsPocketCommand
entries.title: Pocket (Assembly)
entries.summary: This command allows you to create a pocket through several parts with only one operation. This command is available in the assembly document.

entries.name: pocketfromfacesmartedgelistcommand
entries.title: Pocket Edges from Face
entries.summary: - Only inner edges of the pockets are selected, outer edges are not considered..

entries.name: pocketfromloopsmartedgelistcommand
entries.title: Pocket Edges from Loop
entries.summary: - Only edge loops leading to a single face can be selected.

entries.name: pocketsanglesclearancecommand

entries.summary: The selection of the pockets vertical edges is simplified thanks to the two selection help commands available via the button  :

entries.title: This command allows you to locally unfold a (or several) bend(s), in
entries.summary: This command allows you to locally unfold a (or several) bend(s), in order to remove the bend and if required in the neighboring faces, then to rebend it.

entries.title: This command allows you to transform a non

entries.title: Corner relief
entries.summary: This command removes a vertex with a tool to avoid a roll of material during bending.

entries.title: This command rapidly "fills up" the space between two lateral

entries.summary: If no edge needs straightening, the operation does not become invalid, but “does nothing”. This mode allows you to replay a sheet metal part, whatever its geometry. It is particularly useful when defining generics for sheet metal components.

entries.title: This command allows you to add notches on shapes,

entries.summary: This command allows to calculate and visualize by a 2D or 3D sketch, the center of mass trajectory of several rigid groups during a simulation. This command specially allows to see if a simulation center of mass is limited in its supports.....

entries.name: SimulationConfigurationCommand
entries.title: This command allows you to create a configuration of a given simulation, at a t instant.
entries.summary: This command allows you to create a configuration of a given simulation, at a t instant.

entries.title: This command allows you to select the simulation to make current.
entries.summary: This command allows you to select the simulation to make current.

entries.name: DynamicsSimulationCommand
entries.title: This command calculates the simulation before viewing.
entries.summary: - Increasing the number of positions per second and/or the steps by position gives a more precise result but on the other hand it takes more time to calculate.

entries.name: ExportSimulationMeasurementsCommand
entries.title: This command exports the [measurements taken](ViewSimulationMeasurementsCommand.md) to a csv file during a [kinematics](KinematicsSimulationCommand.md) or [dynamic](DynamicsSimulationCommand.md) simulation.
entries.summary: - If the **Auto refresh** mode is active on the [kinematics](KinematicsSimulationCommand.md) or [dynamic](DynamicsSimulationCommand.md) simulations, the Update button is grayed out.

entries.name: KinematicsSimulationCommand
entries.title: This command calculates the simulation before viewing.
entries.summary: - Increasing the number of positions per second and/or the steps by position gives a more precise result but on the other hand it takes more time to calculate.

entries.summary: This command allows you to make current a [kinematic](KinematicsSimulationCommand.md) or existing [dynamic](DynamicsSimulationCommand.md) simulation.

entries.name: AccelerationMeasurementCommand
entries.title: This command measures the acceleration of a rigid group based on its joints during the simulation.

entries.name: AngleMeasurementCommand
entries.title: This command measures the evolution of an angle during the simulation.
entries.summary: The direction of selected axes/edges is important to define the initial angle value.

entries.name: AngularAccelerationMeasurementCommand
entries.title: This command measures the angular acceleration of a rigid group based on its joints during the simulation.

entries.name: AngularVelocityMeasurementCommand
entries.title: This command measures the angular velocity of a rigid group based on its joints during the simulation.

entries.name: DistanceMeasurementCommand
entries.title: This command measures the evolution of a distance during the simulation.

entries.name: EnergyMeasurementCommand
entries.title: This command measures the kinetic or potential energy during the simulation.

entries.name: ForceMeasurementCommand
entries.title: This command measure the force of a rigid group based on its joints during the simulation.

entries.name: ForceMomentMeasurementCommand
entries.title: This command allows you to measure the moment of a rigid group based on its joints during simulation.
entries.summary: This command allows you to measure the moment of a rigid group based on its joints during simulation.

entries.name: VelocityMeasurementCommand
entries.title: This command measures the velocity between 2 points, during the simulation.

entries.name: SimulationPatternCommand

entries.summary: This command allows to define a pattern corresponding to a rigid group displacement during a simulation.

entries.name: PlaySimulationCommand
entries.title: Play Simulation
entries.summary: To record the simulation, use the [Record Simulation](RecordSimulationCommand.md) command.

entries.name: RecordSimulationCommand
entries.title: This command allows you to record a simulation.
entries.summary: This command allows you to record a simulation.

entries.name: ScenarioCommand
entries.title: This command allows you to create a scenario in which several tasks can be inserted and by defining the moment when these tasks must be launched.
entries.summary: This command creates a **Scenarios** sub-folder in the **Mechanisms** folder in the **entity tree**.

entries.name: ForcesDrivingTaskCommand
entries.title: This command defines the different force driving tasks inserted in the scenario. Each force ([punctual](../../Forces/PunctualForceCommand.md), [angular](../../Forces/TorqueForceCommand.md)) may be added to a task, a task can contain one or more forces.
entries.summary: Each task is in relative time and can therefore start at 0 seconds. The time delay occurs at the scenario level. The benefit of this method is to be able to prepare driving sections and start them at the desired time. For example, a "stamp" task has been created. It may be implemented in the scenario at t=10 to start at the 10th second of the scenario.

entries.name: GripChangeTaskCommand
entries.title: This task allows you to "capture" and "release" rigid groups in the scenario.
entries.summary: This task allows you to "capture" and "release" rigid groups in the scenario.

entries.name: JointsDrivingTaskCommand
entries.title: This command defines the different joints driving tasks inserted in the scenario. Joints can be added in a task, a task is able to manage one or more tasks.
entries.summary: Each task is in relative time and can therefore start at 0 seconds. The time delay occurs at the scenario level. The benefit of this method is to be able to prepare driving sections and start them at the desired time. For example, the one-second movement of a part every 5 seconds on a conveyor in continuous operation. A first task will be the continuous movement of the conveyor, the 2nd task will be the movement of the part from 0 to 1 second. The start of the 2nd task will occur at 5 seconds in

entries.name: RigidGroupDrivingTaskCommand
entries.title: This command allows to define a rigid group driving task without having defined all other joints. Displacements will be translations and rotations.
entries.summary: This command allows to define a rigid group driving task without having defined all other joints. Displacements will be translations and rotations.

entries.name: VisibilityChangeTaskCommand
entries.title: This command allows to define a task to hide or show rigid groups.
entries.summary: This command allows to define a task to hide or show rigid groups.

entries.name: TrajectoryCommand
entries.title: This command allows you to view the trajectory from a point linked to a rigid group in relation to another rigid group.
entries.summary: - You cannot modify the trajectory type (basified or not) after the validation.

entries.name: RigidTravelingCommand
entries.title: This command allows to define a movement of a camera fixed on a rigid group. This traveling can be linked to a mechanism simulation.
entries.summary: This command allows to define a movement of a camera fixed on a rigid group. This traveling can be linked to a mechanism simulation.

entries.name: TrackingTravelingCommand
entries.title: This command allows to define a camera fixed on a rigid group and following a mobile rigid group. This traveling can be linked to a mechanism simulation.
entries.summary: This command allows to define a camera fixed on a rigid group and following a mobile rigid group. This traveling can be linked to a mechanism simulation.

entries.name: VariableTravelingCommand
entries.title: This command allows to cumulate several travelings and to define the moment when they have to be started. This traveling can be linked to a mechanism simulation.
entries.summary: This command allows to cumulate several travelings and to define the moment when they have to be started. This traveling can be linked to a mechanism simulation.

entries.summary: The rotation velocity can be used in the Initial velocity drop down window of the [dynamic simulation](../DynamicsSimulationCommand.md).

entries.name: TranslationVelocityCommand
entries.title: This command simulates an initial translation velocity on a rigid group during dynamic simulations.
entries.summary: The translation velocity can be used in the Initial velocity drop down window of the [dynamic simulation](../DynamicsSimulationCommand.md).

entries.summary: - When several curves are displayed on the table, their color may be modified in the **Measurements** folder in the  **entities tree** by right-clicking on the desired measurement and by selecting attributes.

entries.summary: This command allows to manage the stock properties (name, description, part number and major revision) of a document.

entries.summary: The ground is a disc placed on the XY plane of the reference frame on which a design is positioned, allowing to generate cast shadows in a [photorealistic](../../../../../Kernel/UI/RealisticRendering/RealisticRenderingCommand.md) image.

entries.name: PlaneSymmetryCommand
entries.title: This command allows you to indicate that the symmetrical part is equal to itself when forming symmetry in an assembly. If the symmetrical part is not equal to the original part (left part and right part), use the [Mirror Part](../Derivations/CreatePartMirroringCommand.md) function.
entries.summary: The position of the symmetry plane is important when forming symmetry. It is recommended to place it in the middle of the part.

entries.name: RevolutionSymmetryCommand
entries.title: This command allows you to indicate that the symmetrical part is equal to itself in relation to its revolution axis when forming symmetry in an assembly. If the symmetrical part is not equal to the original part (left part and right part), use the [Mirror Part](../Derivations/CreatePartMirroringCommand.md) function. This symmetry is a functional symmetry, which means that the part can only be a revolution part. For example, the axis of a hexagonal screw is a revolution symmetry.
entries.summary: The position of the symmetry axis is important when forming symmetry. It is recommended to place it in the middle of the part.

This referencing is done via the **[Add reference](../../../../../Pdm/UI/Projects/ReferenceProjectCommand.md)** command in the context on the references icon in the project tree.

entries.summary: This command allows to personalize the contents of a view by applying exceptions (lines attributes, unsectionability,...) to entities of this view. entries.title: Forming Area
entries.summary: - This command is only available in the  Modeling stage in the Strip document.

entries.name: formingflangeunfoldingcommand
entries.title: Forming Flange Unfolding
entries.summary: This command allows you to unfold a piece of part by an unfolding by unstamping simulation. The unfolding computation is realized from a mesh of one shape side, projected on a surface.

entries.name: internalformingandholeremovingcommand
entries.title: This command allows you to remove holes and forming inside and / or shared with several faces.
entries.summary: This command allows you to remove holes and forming inside and / or shared with several faces.

entries.name: profileunfoldingtransformcommand
entries.title: This command allows you define the transformation corresponding to the length of a profile, projected on a plane. This transformation will the most often used in the [Areas separation](../ShapeAreaSeparations/shapeareaseparationcommand.md) command, in order to treat the toric bends or bends not actually cylindrical.
entries.summary: This command allows you define the transformation corresponding to the length of a profile, projected on a plane. This transformation will the most often used in the [Areas separation](../ShapeAreaSeparations/shapeareaseparationcommand.md) command, in order to treat the toric bends or bends not actually cylindrical.

entries.name: punchingcommand
entries.title: This command allows you to create punching
entries.summary: This command is only available in the  Modeling stage in the Strip document.

entries.name: shapeareaconnectioncommand
entries.title: This command allows you to connect quickly two shapes together, creating lofted surfaces, and sewing automatically it with the two shapes.
entries.summary: This command allows you to connect quickly two shapes together, creating lofted surfaces, and sewing automatically it with the two shapes.

entries.name: shapeareaseparationcommand
entries.title: This command allows you to separate a shape to strip in two or three parts. It is used to isolate transition areas that can not be treated by unbending.
entries.summary: This command allows you to separate a shape to strip in two or three parts. It is used to isolate transition areas that can not be treated by unbending.

entries.name: surfacesheetmetalcommand
entries.title: This command allows you to transform a solid sheet metal part into a surface one. Only the selected side of the part will be kept. The surface will keep the sheet metal part properties, it will be possible to apply sheet metal dedicated commands to this surface part.
entries.summary: This command allows you to transform a solid sheet metal part into a surface one. Only the selected side of the part will be kept. The surface will keep the sheet metal part properties, it will be possible to apply sheet metal dedicated commands to this surface part.

entries.name: trimandthickencommand
entries.title: This command allows you to trim a surface by a sketch and thicken it in the same operation. It is a complementary command to the [Forming Unfolding](../../Creations/FormingFlangeUnfolding/formingflangeunfoldingcreationcommand.md) one, It will allows you to create the flatten area of the shape to strip.
entries.summary: This command allows you to trim a surface by a sketch and thicken it in the same operation. It is a complementary command to the [Forming Unfolding](../../Creations/FormingFlangeUnfolding/formingflangeunfoldingcreationcommand.md) one, It will allows you to create the flatten area of the shape to strip.

entries.name: strippatterncommand
entries.title: This command creates a linear pattern, following the direction of the selected band and with a spacing distance corresponding to the strip step. The total count of the repetition will correspond to a number of strip stations.
entries.summary: This command creates a linear pattern, following the direction of the selected band and with a spacing distance corresponding to the strip step. The total count of the repetition will correspond to a number of strip stations.

entries.name: includepartorassemblycommand
entries.title: This command allows you to include a Sheet
entries.summary: This command allows you to include a Sheet metal part or a sub assembly in an assembly document. This component inclusion complies with a positioning.

entries.name: inclusiondragcommand
entries.title: Inclusion drag command
entries.summary: This command allows you to include a **Sheet metal part** or a **sub assembly** in an assembly document. This component inclusion complies with a positioning.

entries.name: shapestostripcommand
entries.title: This command allows you to define the shapes to strip used in your Strip design.
entries.summary: This command allows you to define the shapes to strip used in your Strip design.

entries.name: shapetostripreplacingcommand
entries.title: This command allows you to replace a Shape to strip by its plane projection, or by a new shape. In flattening mode, it ensures that the flattening result is perfectly plane, with the same thickness than the shape to strip.
entries.summary: This command allows you to replace a Shape to strip by its plane projection, or by a new shape. In flattening mode, it ensures that the flattening result is perfectly plane, with the same thickness than the shape to strip.

entries.name: stationshapecommand
entries.title: Station Shape
entries.summary: This command allows you to create the station shapes that will compose the different strip stations. It is a photograph of the shapes to strip at the moment of the launch of the command.

entries.name: stationspreparationshapesmanagementcommand
entries.title: Station Shapes Management
entries.summary: This command allows you to visualize the creation process in the future strip, step by step. It is possible to compare the geometry between the different station shapes.

entries.name: mergedstripcommand
entries.title: This command allows you to unite the strip skeleton with the strip station shapes. Only one shape will be created in the Merged Strip Representation.
entries.summary: This command allows you to unite the strip skeleton with the strip station shapes. Only one shape will be created in the Merged Strip Representation.

entries.name: stripoptimizingcommand
entries.title: This command is used to find the best possible placement of a part in a strip. Several types of placements will be analyzed simultaneously.
entries.summary: The more solutions to analyze, the more expensive the calculation time will be. It may be useful to make a first calculation with "coarse" parameters and then refine it.

entries.name: punchingnotchcommand
entries.title: This command allows you predefined notches to be added to the punching contours.
entries.summary: This command allows you predefined notches to be added to the punching contours.

entries.name: stationpositioncommand
entries.title: This command allows you to modify the position of the selected station shape.
entries.summary: This command allows you to modify the position of the selected station shape.

entries.name: stationsinformationmanagementcommand
entries.title: Stations Information
entries.summary: - This command does not create an operation in the operations tree. - It allows you to fill in the [Information](../../../../Kernel/UI/Commands/OperationInformationCommand.md) for all the stations via the same command.

entries.name: stationsinsertioncommand
entries.title: This command allows you to add stations to a strip, Before or After the selected reference station, without editing the operation to create the [Strip](stripcommand.md).

entries.name: stripcommand
entries.title: This command allows you to create the
entries.summary: Only one strip can be created in a Strip document. Re-launch the command edits the strip previously created.

entries.name: hidecomponentscommand
entries.title: Hide Components
entries.summary: - Only one operation is created per view. If a Hide components operation has already been created on this view, it will be edited.

entries.name: toolingviewcommand
entries.title: This command allows you to create the main views of a Mold, the A side and B side views, the main views of a Progressive Die, the Die Side, Stripper and Punch Side views for example.
entries.summary: This command allows you to create the main views of a Mold, the A side and B side views, the main views of a Progressive Die, the Die Side, Stripper and Punch Side views for example.

entries.name: drillingsmarkingcommand

entries.summary: When the command is launched, the font, size and direction settings are chosen based on a first drilling. Then these settings are automatically associated with all the selected drillings. The positions of each marking can be adjusted at each drilling.

entries.name: passingprocesscommand
entries.title: This command is used to create the passing processes for the different tools chosen. The processes carried out are subtractions with clearances in the parts that collide with this tool.
entries.summary: In a Progressive Die study, in order to create in the best conditions the passing process of a punch, it is recommended to have made the corresponding cutting die before launching this command.

entries.name: passingprocessdefaultclearancedefinitioncommand
entries.title: This command is used to define the clearance to be associated with the parts to be operated in the [Passing Process](passingprocesscommand.md) command.
entries.summary: - Since the clearances applied in the same plate are often the same, it is very useful to define the  Passing Process Clearance on the plates before carrying out the [Passing Processes](passingprocesscommand.md) in the tooling.

entries.title: This contextual command allows to basify (break the associativity) the selected entity. The result is the same than after an import. There is no more operations history. The entity can't be modified after a basification. For example, if an offset plane is basified, it will not be possible to edit it and change the offset value.
entries.summary: This operation is irreversible.

entries.name: UpdateAllCommand
entries.title: Update all
entries.summary: - In order to improve performances, updating the analysis elements (mass, volume, center of mass, etc...) can be disabled in order not to recalculate this information with each modification. In this case, updating the document is done manually if required.

entries.name: UpdateAutoCommand
entries.title: This mode allows automatic updating of the entities being modified to be enabled or disabled.
entries.summary: - During the modification of complex parts, assemblies, drafts, the **automatic updating** of documents can reduce performances, it is then advised to disable this mode. Example: An assembly of 4000 parts is drafted, modifications are made on this assembly, it is advised to disable the **automatic updating** so as not to recalculate the draft immediately.

entries.name: UpdateCommand
entries.title: This command allows the documents to be updated manually.
entries.summary: In some cases, in order to improve performances, [Automatic updating](UpdateAutoCommand.md) is disabled. In this case, the command is located in the graphic, and the update is done manually, if needed.

entries.summary: A document made with an old version of TopSolid may not have all information which it could have if it has been created with the latest version of TopSolid. This command allows to upgrade to the newest version.

entries.name: UpgradeOperationCommand
entries.title: An operation done with an old TopSolid version may not have all information it should have if it was created with the latest TopSolid version. This contextual command allows to upgrade it to the latest version.
entries.summary: An operation done with an old TopSolid version may not have all information it should have if it was created with the latest TopSolid version. This contextual command allows to upgrade it to the latest version.

entries.name: VirtualDocumentCommand
entries.title: This command allows you to convert a document into a virtual document. A virtual document is a document that should not be a document, but is required to create other documents. A virtual document is not proposed during a search or when selecting a document in a dialog. For example, to do a profile family, the main part is only used for parametrization but must not be used in an assembly. By changing this part to a virtual document,  of parts, the base is a part. If it has not to be used, it should be transformed into a virtual document, only family instances of this profile will be proposed when searching or using the Profile command.

entries.summary: This command allows an image to be included in a title block or in the detailing of a view.

entries.name: ArcOrCircleCreationCommand
entries.title: This command allows you to create an arc or circle from two or 3 points (peaks) of the sketch. It will be in particular used to complete (close) the elements created by sketch operations so as for example to obtain a closed contour allowing a solid to be built during an extrusion.
entries.summary: This command allows you to create an arc or circle from two or 3 points (peaks) of the sketch. It will be in particular used to complete (close) the elements created by sketch operations so as for example to obtain a closed contour allowing a solid to be built during an extrusion.

entries.name: BooleanCommand

entries.summary: This command allows to unit, substract or intersect a profil or a section with others profils/sections of the same sketch or sketches previously created.

entries.title: This command allows you to create a chamfer on the vertex of a sketch.
entries.summary: Upon selecting the profile, TopSolid searches for the nearest vertex to apply the chamfer. The vertex and the segment are then automatically selected.

entries.summary: If a sketch is created with the sewing manual mode (option available in **2D or 3D sketch >[Options...](../Options/EditSketchOptionSetCommand.md)**), this command allows to transform a batch of segments to profile. This can be used in an extrusion, trim, etc.

entries.summary: This command allows a 2D sketch element (profile or point) according to a chosen pattern to be repeated (duplication with link).

entries.name: SectionCreationCommand
entries.title: This command allows you to create a section between different profiles. This section can be used in the [Extrusion](../../../D3/Shapes/Extruded/ExtrudedCommand.md) command to extrude the profiles included in the section without taking into account other possible sketch profiles.
entries.summary: This command allows you to create a section between different profiles. This section can be used in the [Extrusion](../../../D3/Shapes/Extruded/ExtrudedCommand.md) command to extrude the profiles included in the section without taking into account other possible sketch profiles.

entries.name: ExtendAxisCommand
entries.title: This command allows to extent an axis. By default the axis length is automatically defined regarding the selected geometry. If the geometry changes, the axis length changes too. By using this command, the axis length will be modifiable manually, and will not change if the geometry changes.

entries.name: PartialPatternCommand
entries.title: This command allows to create a sub-pattern of an existing patter in order to excluded some of the instances using a specific period. The most common case is a chain and its links. The links are repeated on a curve, but there is two types of links in a chain. If we use the command [profile pattern](../Shapes/Patterns/profilepatterncommand.md) with a step multiplied by 2, the position of the link will be wrong along the circular path around the gearwheel. In this case, we can use the partial pattern to compute the complete repetition end to end, but by extracting one in every two instance.

The profiles can have several positioning points regarding the profile type (Bar L, Bar U, others, ...)When using the [provide function](../../Functions/ProvideFunctionCommand.md) command from a part, a **Functions** folder is created in the entities tree and its list of points can be [edited and completed](../../Publishings/PublishingsFolderEditingCommand.md).

entries.name: EdgePickingFilterCommand

entries.name: ExtractSketchCommand
entries.title: When a sketch is used by a function [Extruded](../Extruded/ExtrudedCommand.md)), the sketch is "absorbed" by the function in the operation tree. If the extruded is removed, the sketch is as well. If the extruded must be removed without deleting the sketch, this command can extract the sketch from the operation. This command can also be used to extract the sketch so that it can be used a second time with another operation.
entries.summary: When a sketch is used by a function [Extruded](../Extruded/ExtrudedCommand.md)), the sketch is "absorbed" by the function in the operation tree. If the extruded is removed, the sketch is as well. If the extruded must be removed without deleting the sketch, this command can extract the sketch from the operation. This command can also be used to extract the sketch so that it can be used a second time with another operation.

In fact, surfaces from an IGES are b-spline surfaces (not true planar or cylindrical faces in the mathematical sense of the term). Simplification will therefore result in a lighter value, authorizing the use of command that require planar or cylindrical faces as reference.

entries.summary: Once the sketch has been made editable, the import operation is replaced by a Building and Solving operation same as usual sketch operation created inside TopSolid.

entries.name: OrientatePerspectiveViewCommand
entries.title: This command orients the current view of the sketch in perspective in relation to the sketch frame.
entries.summary: If your document is [splitted](../../../../../WG/Commands/Views/SplitViewCommand.md) in several views, only the current view will be modified.

entries.name: OrientateTopViewCommand
entries.title: This command orients the current view of the sketch to a top view in relation to the sketch frame.
entries.summary: If your document is [splitted](../../../../../WG/Commands/Views/SplitViewCommand.md) in several views, only the current view will be modified.

entries.summary: This setting is not taken into account when the sketch is edited.

entries.name: ZoomToFitCommand
entries.title: This command allows you to [zoom to fit](../../../../../WG/Commands/Views/ZoomToFitCommand.md) the sketch in the current view.
entries.summary: If your document is [splitted](../../../../../WG/Commands/Views/SplitViewCommand.md) in several views, only the current view will be modified.

entries.name: SwitchIntegerParameterCommand

entries.summary: This command allows you to create a switch parameter. The value for this parameter will be an integer. This type of parameter allows you to make a link between a [table parameter](TableRealParameterCommand.md) and [enumeration functions](SmartUserEnumerationParameterCommand.md).

entries.name: SwitchRealParameterCommand
entries.title: This command allows you to create a switch parameter. This type of parameter allows you to make a link between a [table parameter](TableRealParameterCommand.md) and [enumeration functions](SmartUserEnumerationParameterCommand.md).
entries.summary: Example of use: For a screw function document, the enumeration parameters provide the different types of heads. A C1 [tabulated parameter](TableRealParameterCommand.md) provides the diameter of the spot facing for a Cylindrical head. A C2 [tabulated parameter](TableRealParameterCommand.md) provides the diameter of the spot facing for the countersunk head H. The switch parameter makes a link between the different types of heads and the corresponding spot facing values.

entries.name: SaveScreenshotCommand
entries.title: This command allows to create a basic picture file with the render displayed on screen, without option and without using the [realistic rendering](../../UI/RealisticRendering/RealisticRenderingCommand.md) command which needs a paid license but which is much more efficient and has a lot of rendering options.
entries.summary: This command allows to create a basic picture file with the render displayed on screen, without option and without using the [realistic rendering](../../UI/RealisticRendering/RealisticRenderingCommand.md) command which needs a paid license but which is much more efficient and has a lot of rendering options.

entries.name: SelectAreaCommand
entries.title: Select by area
entries.summary: - Only the items included in the zone will be selected.

entries.name: SelectCurveCommand
entries.title: This mode allows you to select all the items intersected by a curve.
entries.summary: - Point type items cannot be selected with this mode.

entries.name: ShowPropertiesCommand
entries.title: This command available from assembly document only allows to display part properties.
entries.summary: This command available from assembly document only allows to display part properties.

entries.name: TreeSelectionEchoArrowCommand
entries.title: When you select an element from the entities or operations tree, this mode allows you to display an arrow that point out the element in the graphical area.
entries.summary: When you select an element from the entities or operations tree, this mode allows you to display an arrow that point out the element in the graphical area.

entries.name: UnsafeItemPickingFilterCommand
entries.title: This mode allows to filter automatic detection of faces whose hooking could not be assured throughout the part life cycle.
entries.summary: This filter can be cumulated with the [Faces filter](../../UI/D2/Sketches/Sketching/Modes/FacePickingFilterModeCommand.md), [Edges filter](../../UI/D3/Shapes/Commands/EdgePickingFilterCommand.md) and with one of these 3: [Filter by color](../../UI/Filters/ColorFilterCommand.md), [Filter by properties](../../../Cad/Design/UI/Filters/PropertiesFilterCommand.md), [Filter by entities](../../UI/Filters/EntitiesFilterCommand.md).

entries.name: DeleteViewCommand
entries.title: Delete view
entries.summary: When only one view is remaining on the screen, this command is not available.

entries.name: FourViewsCommand
entries.title: This command allows to split into four views, the current document display. It is then possible to have simultaneously a 3d, a left, a front and a top view of the same document.
entries.summary: - It is possible to re size the views by dragging the separator.

entries.name: LockVerticalCommand
entries.title: Lock vertical
entries.summary: The Z-axis of the absolute frame is locked when the view is dynamically rotated.

entries.name: LockViewCommand
entries.title: This command allows you to lock a view.
entries.summary: - When a view is locked, zoom, orientations and cameras icons are grayed.

entries.name: OneViewCommand
entries.title: This command allows you to quickly switch your graphic zone into a view.

entries.name: PanViewCommand
entries.title: This command allows you to dynamically move your view into translation.
entries.summary: The right mouse button also allows to use this command regardless the current command.

entries.name: RotateViewCommand
entries.title: This command allows you to dynamically rotate your view around a point.
entries.summary: The middle button also allows you to use this same command regardless of the function currently in use.

entries.name: SpinViewCommand
entries.title: This command allows you to dynamically rotate your view around an axis.
entries.summary: The rotation in made around the normal axis of the center of the view.

entries.name: SplitViewCommand
entries.title: This command splits a view horizontally or vertically into two.
entries.summary: - By moving the mouse cursor on the vertical boundaries of the view, the dashed line is horizontal. By moving the mouse cursor on the horizontal boundaries of the view, the dashed line is vertical.

entries.name: ThreeViewsCommand
entries.title: This command allows to split into three views, the current document display. It is then possible to have simultaneously a 3d view a left and a top view of the same document.
entries.summary: - It is possible to resize the views by dragging the separator.

entries.name: TwoViewsCommand
entries.title: This command allows to split into two views, the current document display. It is then possible to have simultaneously a 3d view and a top view of the same document.
entries.summary: - It is possible to resize the views by dragging the separator.

entries.name: ZoomBoxCommand
entries.title: This zoom allows you to focus on a zone of the current view by enclosing the area to zoom on.

entries.name: ZoomToFitCommand
entries.title: This zoom allows you to resize a screen view so as to fully visualize all the project items.
entries.summary: - This command does not require any interaction from the user. The zoom to fit is calculated on the current view upon activating the command.

entries.name: DraftingMultiplePrintCommand
entries.title: This command allows to print several drafts of a project or a folder.
entries.summary: This command allows to print several drafts of a project or a folder.

entries.name: MultiplePrintCommand
entries.title: This command allows to print all non converted file (PDF, PNG, JPG, ... Word, Excel document, ...) and printable directly from the Windows explorer. It selects also all drafts of the different sub-folders.
entries.summary: This command allows to print all non converted file (PDF, PNG, JPG, ... Word, Excel document, ...) and printable directly from the Windows explorer. It selects also all drafts of the different sub-folders.

entries.name: PrintCommand
entries.title: This command prints a document or a view.
entries.summary: The higher the resolution, the better the quality the result will be, but to the detriment of printing time.

entries.name: AddToSelectionCommand
entries.title: This mode allows to add items to a current selection
entries.summary: This mode allows to add items to a current selection

entries.name: IntersectWithSelectionCommand
entries.title: This mode allows to keep only the elements that intersect an existing
entries.summary: This mode allows to keep only the elements that intersect an existing selection and the new selection.

entries.name: RemoveFromSelectionCommand
entries.title: This command allows to
entries.summary: This command allows to

entries.name: AboutCommand
entries.title: TopSolid
entries.summary: - This information can be useful for the support.

entries.name: AddInsCommand
entries.title: This command activates or deactivates the additional modules installed with **TopSolid**depending on the licenses.
entries.summary: The modifications are taken into account upon the next startup of **TopSolid**.

entries.name: AutomationGuideCommand
entries.title: This command allows to start the guide that explains how to drive TopSolid from another application to automate some frequent tasks.
entries.summary: This command allows to start the guide that explains how to drive TopSolid from another application to automate some frequent tasks.

entries.name: AutomationHelpCommand
entries.title: This command allows to start help to explain how available commands work to be able to drive TopSolid from another software to automate some frequent tasks.
entries.summary: This command allows to start help to explain how available commands work to be able to drive TopSolid from another software to automate some frequent tasks.

entries.name: AutomationWhatsNewCommand
entries.title: This command opens the Automation What's New document.
entries.summary: - A Pdf reader has to be installed. Several free readers exist.

entries.name: CheckForUpdatesCommand
entries.title: Search for updates
entries.summary: - During the update, **TopSolid** must be shut down. If TopSolid is started, you will be asked whether or not to stop it.

entries.name: CloseAllDocumentWindowsCommand
entries.title: This command closes all open documents and saves the last modifications if any.
entries.summary: This command is also accessible directly from the popup menu of the **documents tab**.

entries.name: CloseDocumentCommand
entries.title: This command closes the current document and saves the last modifications if any.
entries.summary: This command is accessible by clicking on the "X" displayed in the upper right-hand of the document or directly from the popup menu of the **documents tab**.

entries.name: CopyMenuCommand
entries.title: This command allows you to copy items from operations so that you can paste them later in a [non-associative](PasteMenuCommand.md) way.

entries.name: CustomizeApplicationCommand
entries.title: This command allows you to define or delete keyboard shortcuts or icons
entries.summary: This command allows you to define or delete keyboard shortcuts or icons in the toolbars.

entries.name: customizecontextmenucommand
entries.title: customize (context menu)
entries.summary: In this list, you can only see the commands that are compatible with the entity type from which the customizing has been lunch.

entries.name: DialogPaneOnDocumentCommand
entries.title: This command displays the dialog of the different functions in the documents window and not in the tree. In this case, the dialog will still be visible on the document, as long as the corresponding function is not validated or canceled.
entries.summary: When the background of the icon is colored, the option is activated.

entries.name: editdatagridviewfiltercommand
entries.title: This command allows to filter the contents of a list (BOM displayed
entries.summary: This command allows to filter the contents of a list (BOM displayed in the [Parts tree](../../../Cad/Design/UI/Views/Parts/CharacteristicPartsControl.md), [Project](../../../Pdm/UI/Projects/ProjectsCommand.md) manager, ...).

entries.name: ExitCommand
entries.title: This command allows you to exit the TopSolid application.
entries.summary: The documents that are modified, not saved or not put back into the vault have an asterisk next to the name in the tab of the window.

entries.name: ExportCadianCommand
entries.title: This command allows you to export a **draft document** to a format compatible with **CADIAN** (format *.dwg).
entries.summary: This export is only available if TopSolid is running in Korean.

entries.name: ExportCommand
entries.title: Export Document with Conversion
entries.summary: You cannot export unless one or more documents are open. It is the current document that will be exported.

entries.name: FullScreenCommand
entries.title: This mode allows to switch TopSolid in a full screen mode. The [system bar](../../../../UserInterface/UserInterface.md), the banner, context tabs and the different trees are not displayed.
entries.summary: This mode allows to switch TopSolid in a full screen mode. The [system bar](../../../../UserInterface/UserInterface.md), the banner, context tabs and the different trees are not displayed.

entries.name: HelpCommand
entries.title: Help

entries.name: ImportLibraryCommand
entries.title: This command allows you to reconstruct a **TopSolid 7** library from **TopSolid V6** library files.
entries.summary: This command will create **explicit families** without [generic documents](../../UI/Families/Commands/DefineFamilyGenericCommand.md). These families only contain one code column with all V6 component codes (component settings are not found in this document).

entries.name: ImportOpenPackageCommand
entries.title: This command allows to import Openpackage document( extension is .TopOpnPkg).
entries.summary: This command allows to import Openpackage document( extension is .TopOpnPkg).

entries.title: This command allows to display the installation guide for **TopSolid 7** and **TopSolid'Pdm Server.**
entries.summary: This command allows to display the installation guide for **TopSolid 7** and **TopSolid'Pdm Server.**

entries.name: LibraryDesignerGuideCommand
entries.title: This command allows to display the guide to design general libraries, standards or from manufacturers.
entries.summary: This command allows to display the guide to design general libraries, standards or from manufacturers.

entries.name: LockDockedWindowsCommand
entries.title: This command prevents an unfortunated click to modify the setting or the position of windows defined by user. Windows can not be moved/hidden/folded anymore, but they can be resized. Projects are excluded from this rule and can be closed.
entries.summary: This command prevents an unfortunated click to modify the setting or the position of windows defined by user. Windows can not be moved/hidden/folded anymore, but they can be resized. Projects are excluded from this rule and can be closed.

entries.name: ModifyDocumentCommand
entries.title: This command allows to make modifiable a document opened as read-only mode ([consult](../Pdm/Commands/ConsultDocumentCommand.md)).
entries.summary: This command allows to make modifiable a document opened as read-only mode ([consult](../Pdm/Commands/ConsultDocumentCommand.md)).

entries.title: This command creates a new window with the current document.
entries.summary: - A new window opens with the current document.

entries.name: PageSetupCommand
entries.title: This command allows you to set up the page before printing.

entries.name: PasteMenuCommand
entries.title: This command allows you to paste, in a non-associative manner, [previously copied](CopyMenuCommand.md) items.

entries.name: PreviewCommand
entries.title: This mode activates or deactivates the preview of the functions during the creation or modification of shapes.
entries.summary: During the creation or modification of functions in very complex parts, the preview display can slow down computer performance, therefore deactivating this mode is advised.

entries.name: PreviewOffCommand
entries.title: This mode allows to deactivate preview in certain operations.
entries.summary: This mode allows to deactivate preview in certain operations.

entries.name: PreviewOnCommand
entries.title: This mode allows to deactivate preview in certain operations.
entries.summary: This mode allows to deactivate preview in certain operations.

entries.name: RedoCommand
entries.title: This command restores previously deleted commands.
entries.summary: The last canceled command is displayed at the top of the list; commands must be restored from this last command.

entries.name: RemoteAssistanceCommand
entries.title: This command allows the TopSolid support to have a remote access to your computer.
entries.summary: This command allows the TopSolid support to have a remote access to your computer.

entries.name: ResetApplicationSettingsCommand
entries.title: Manage Settings
entries.summary: - Resetting destroys all customized settings depending on the selected options. If need be, you then may have to redo all of the customized settings. These customized settings also concern the remote PDM connections and the locations of vaults.

entries.name: SaveAllCommand
entries.title: Save all
entries.summary: This command is accessible with the Ctrl + Shift + S keys, it can also be launched from the File menu of the TopSolid button menu or directly from the popup menu of the documents tab.

entries.name: SaveCommand
entries.title: Save document
entries.summary: This command is accessible with the CTRL + S keys, it can also be launched from the File menu of the TopSolid button or directly from the popup menu of the documents tab.

entries.title: TopSolid Assistant
entries.summary: - On first use, select the icon available in the lower message in order to be able to enter your question.

entries.name: TopSolidDiagnosticsCommand
entries.title: TopSolid Diagnostics
entries.summary: After the analysis, the **Help** button available in the right-hand window (select an item in the left tree) will provide you with more information about this diagnostic tool.

entries.name: TopSolidForumAccessCommand
entries.title: This command allows you to connect to the TopSolid'Forum on [http://forum.topsolid.com/](http://forum.topsolid.com/)
entries.summary: This command opens the [http://forum.topsolid.com/](http://forum.topsolid.com/) site in your web browser.

entries.summary: - A pdf reader has to be installed. There are several free readers available.

entries.summary: The command used last is displayed at the top of the list, the cancellation of the commands must be made from this last command.

entries.name: UpdatesNotesCommand

entries.summary: A PDF file reader must be installed to view this document.

For example, after a material update, the opening of a part using this material proposes a redirection to this new material. By using this command, the document will be opened

entries.title: This command allows to definitively purge documents from the recycle bin.
entries.summary: This command allows to definitively purge documents from the recycle bin.

entries.title: This contextual command allows you to rename an object or project.
entries.summary: This command is only available from the popup menu in the project tree.

entries.title: This command allows you to cancel the deletion of the selected documents when they have been deleted and then [checked in](checkincommand.md).
entries.summary: It is possible to make multiple selections by holding the CTRL key while selecting before canceling the deletion.

entries.title: This command allows you to return to the state of the document as it was before being saved.

entries.title: This command allows you to make a copy of the existing document by saving it in the same project under another name.
entries.summary: A synchronized document (a part [created in place](../../../Cad/Design/UI/Commands/CreateInPlacePartCommand.md) in the assembly) is not managed by the **Save Document As** command.

entries.name: savecommand
entries.title: This command allows you to save objects in your [workspace](../Overview.md#Workspace).
entries.summary: - When an object can be saved, its name in the project tree is followed by an asterisk. It will disappear when the file is saved and will only re-appear after the object has been modified.

entries.name: ShowObjectsBeforeActionCommand
entries.title: This window summarizes the requested action before its validation and shows the effects of the change. For example, when storing a project into the vault, all the objects in this project will be listed and will show in the status column whether the action is possible or not. In case it is not possible, the reason why will be displayed and you will not be able to validate before resolving the problem.
entries.summary: If none of the objects has an impossible status, the action can be validated. If at least one document has an impossible status, you have to cancel and resolve the problem before retrying the action.

entries.name: UndoPendingChangesCommand
entries.title: This command allows you to cancel check-out for edit or cancel the deletion of a part.
entries.summary: - You can only cancel taking out of the vault for objects known by the PDM and taken out of the vault (they show a red check mark in the project tree). Following the canceling, they are again displayed with the blue padlock

entries.name: ConnectionAsCommand
entries.title: Connection as
entries.summary: This allows, for example, to check-in the documents of an absent person (user who has left the company, employee on vacation, student, etc...).

entries.name: ConnectionCommand
entries.title: This command allows you to connect to the Pdm server as well as configure
entries.summary: - The  authentification to TopSolid by using the windows account allows to simplify the TopSolid Pdm administration. Instead of duplicating all information from the Active directory to TopSolid (Name, First name, E-mail address, groups, ...) TopSolid directly asks the domain controller.

entries.title: New document (Project)

entries.title: Archive and restore
entries.summary: - Projects are archived asynchronously and stored in a specific folder defined in the vault Advanced management window of the PDM (Local or Server). Each archive has an identifier recognized by the issuing PDM. Archived project files are then automatically deleted from the vault. - In the project manager, a new icon at the top right of the window shows the

entries.name: CancelValidateMajorRevisionCommand
entries.title: This command allows you to cancel the validation of a document based on specific conditions.

entries.name: ConvertDocumentCommand
entries.title: Convert file
entries.summary: Importing a file requires converting the data. This operation requires system resources and can take some time depending on the size of the file.

entries.name: CreateNewDocumentCommand
entries.title: New document
entries.summary: - The **Recent** tab contains the latest types of documents created.

entries.name: exportdocumentwithconversioncommand
entries.title: Export Document with Conversion
entries.summary: - Data must be converted to export a file. This operation requires system resources and can take some time depending on the size of the file.

entries.name: exportdocumentwithoutconversioncommand
entries.title: Export Document without Conversion

entries.name: ImportFileAsNewMinorRevisionCommand
entries.title: This command is useful, for example, when you receive successive versions of a small technical document. You import the first version using the "Import without conversion" command from a Word file. The subsequent versions are imported by launching the command "Import file as a new minor revision" on the object first created when you imported without conversion.
entries.summary: This command is useful, for example, when you receive successive versions of a small technical document. You import the first version using the "Import without conversion" command from a Word file. The subsequent versions are imported by launching the command "Import file as a new minor revision" on the object first created when you imported without conversion.

entries.name: ImportWithConversionCommand
entries.title: Import file with conversion
entries.summary: - Importing a file requires converting the data. This operation requires system resources and can take some time depending on the size of the file.

entries.name: ImportWithoutConversionCommand
entries.title: Import file without conversion
entries.summary: - You can drag and drop files from the Windows Explorer to the desired project or folder.

entries.name: MakeObsoleteCommand
entries.title: This command allows to make obsolete a validated document, so that it is no longer used.
entries.summary: To cancel this command, you have to [validate](validatemajorrevisioncommand.md) again the revision.

entries.name: MinorRevisionTextsCommand
entries.title: Revision texts
entries.summary: You can mix letters, numbers and special characters in the major revision text but the minor revision text should contain only numbers or letters.

entries.name: MultiExportDocumentWithConversionCommand
entries.title: Export Multiple Documents with Conversion
entries.summary: - Options are general to all files of the same type. If the options must be different (different versions, some in DXF format, others in DWG format), different exports must be performed.

entries.name: MultiImportWithConversionCommand
entries.title: Import Multiple Files with Conversion
entries.summary: - Options are general to all files of the same type. If options must be different (for example, opening certain DWG files as parts and others as drafts), different imports must be performed.

entries.name: MultiImportWithoutConversionCommand
entries.title: This command allows you to import several CAD files without conversion, but also images, videos, Word, Excel documents,.... It allows you to reference the files in the PDM to track or [convert](ConvertDocumentCommand.md) them later.
entries.summary: - You can drag and drop files from the Windows Explorer to the desired project or folder.

entries.name: OpenDocumentCommand
entries.title: This command allows you to open a TopSolid document (part, assembly, draft, material, family, etc.) in your workspace.
entries.summary: This command only opens the document. if no change is made to the document, it can be closed without saving. The document is in consultation mode until a change is made, in which case, it becomes a modification. Rotating and zooming are not considered as changes.

entries.name: RebuildFamilyInstanceDocumentCommand
entries.title: This contextual command allows to regenerate a family instance to update its information.
entries.summary: This contextual command allows to regenerate a family instance to update its information.

entries.name: RestoreAsLastRevisionCommand
entries.title: This command allows you to "come back" to a preceding [minor revision](../Overview.md#Indexes) from the project tree document and redefine this revision as the latest revision. For example, a modification on a part was placed in the vault, but the modification is not required.
entries.summary: This command allows you to "come back" to a preceding [minor revision](../Overview.md#Indexes) from the project tree document and redefine this revision as the latest revision. For example, a modification on a part was placed in the vault, but the modification is not required.

entries.name: RestorePreviousRevisionCommand

entries.summary: - To be validated, an object has to be in the vault (blue padlock before the object in the project tree).

entries.name: CopyCommand
entries.title: This command allows you to copy one or more documents in the same or another project.
entries.summary: - The documents are in the memory. You can [paste](PasteCommand.md) them in the same project or a different one.

entries.summary: This command allows you to cut one or more documents in the same or another project.

entries.summary: - You can export multiple files from the same project in the same package file by using multiple selection (holding the CTRL key down while selecting).

entries.name: ImportPackageAsAssociativeCopyInNodeObjectCommand
entries.title: This command allows you to import a package as read-only. An import folder will be created in the project tree. No notification can be made by the users on the files contained in this folder.
entries.summary: - A folder named Import folder is automatically created in the project tree. The documents are read-only and cannot be edited.

entries.name: ImportPackageAsCopyCommand
entries.title: Import project as copy
entries.summary: - The project and the documents created are completely independent from those exported, for example, by importing the project in the same vault as the initial project.

entries.name: ImportPackageAsCopyInNodeObjectCommand
entries.title: Import Package (copy)
entries.summary: - The project and documents created are completely independent of those exported. It is an import of project as distinct copy.

entries.name: ImportPackageAsDistinctCopyInNodeObjectCommand
entries.title: This command allows you to import exported documents as a package. All the necessary references for these documents will also be imported. This command is only available from the popup menu on a folder or project.
entries.summary: - The project and documents created are completely independent of those exported. It is an import of project as distinct copy.

entries.name: ImportPackageAsReplicationCommand
entries.title: Import project
entries.summary: To use import by replication, it is imperative that all documents be [checked in](../Commands/checkincommand.md) before [exporting the package](ExportPackageCommand.md). If this condition is not correct, it is [import project as distinct copy](ImportPackageAsCopyCommand.md) command which will be automatically

entries.name: ImportPackageInImportFolderCommand
entries.title: This mode works only with [Import as an associative copy](ImportPackageAsAssociativeCopyInNodeObjectCommand.md). It is only available on an import folder previously created with Import as an associative copy. It updates the contents of the import folder.
entries.summary: Update by importing is the only way to update documents imported in associative mode

entries.name: PasteCommand
entries.title: This command allows you to paste one or more documents that were previously [copied](CopyCommand.md) or [cut](CutCommand.md) from the same project or a different one.
entries.summary: - The documents must have previously been copied or cut. The copied documents will be renamed Copy of..., the cut objects will be moved.

entries.name: SpecialPasteCommand
entries.title: This command allows to paste a document previously [copied](CopyCommand.md) or [cut](CutCommand.md) from the same project or a different one with or without referenced documents.
entries.summary: This command allows to paste a document previously [copied](CopyCommand.md) or [cut](CutCommand.md) from the same project or a different one with or without referenced documents.

entries.name: NewStandardDocumentTemplatesProjectCommand
entries.title: This command allows to create a new project with [templates](../Overview.md#Templates) of standard documents. When created a new document, templates are proposed (for example, the different templates for a draft). These templates are either in the project or either in the templates projects (**My templates** or **Company templates**). This command allows to create a new project of templates which can be delivered to customers or partners.
entries.summary: This command allows to create a new project with [templates](../Overview.md#Templates) of standard documents. When created a new document, templates are proposed (for example, the different templates for a draft). These templates are either in the project or either in the templates projects (**My templates** or **Company templates**). This command allows to create a new project of templates which can be delivered to customers or partners.

entries.name: NewUniversalLibraryCommand
entries.title: This command allows to create a new universal library. It contains the needed dictionaries to be used with several languages, like the TopSolid standard libraries. For example, the screws are automatically translated to "vis" if TopSolid is started in French.
entries.summary: This command allows to create a new universal library. It contains the needed dictionaries to be used with several languages, like the TopSolid standard libraries. For example, the screws are automatically translated to "vis" if TopSolid is started in French.

entries.name: OpenCompanyProjectTemplatesCommand
entries.title: This command allows you to open the company project templates to complete, modify or delete one or more project templates common to all users. A project template is a project used as a base for newly created projects. If a project template contains folders and files, these will be automatically created in a [new project](NewProjectCommand.md) that uses this template.

entries.summary: You can add a new column (Project name, Author or other...) by right clicking a column title.

entries.name: DocumentReferencesCommand
entries.title: This command allows you to find an object's references. For example, an assembly's references are the parts and sub-assemblies it is made of. The reverse function is [Back references](DocumentReferencesCommand.md). The different functions are explained below.

entries.name: NotUpToDateReferenceCommand
entries.title: This command allows you to update assemblies that do not reference the
entries.summary: - Checking **Select all minor revisions** allows you to check all the documents in the list. If you uncheck this option, all documents will be unchecked.

entries.name: RedirectDocumentReferencesCommand
entries.title: This command allows you to redirect the current documents references. For example, if an assembly uses parts that have evolved (P1.A has become P1.B), you can redirect the assembly to part P1.B instead of P1.A.
entries.summary: You can click in the last column to manually select the desired major version of the object, or the last minor revision of the last major revision. For example, if P1's history is P1.A.1 (minor), P1.A.2 (minor), P1.A (major), P1.B.1 (minor), P2.B.2 (minor), you can select P1.A and P1.B.2

entries.name: RedirectReferencesCommand
entries.title: This command allows you to redirect references to the last major revision. For example, an assembly consists of 2 validated parts (Part1.A and Part2.A). These 2 parts have changed independently from the assembly (Part1.A has become Part1.Ben and Part2.A has become Part2.Edo); you can redirect the assembly to their latest revision. The assembly will then reference Part1.A and Part2.E.
entries.summary: This command can be used in multiple selection or from a project.

entries.name: PdmObjectSecurityCommand
entries.title: This contextual command allows you to modify the security of the project,
entries.summary: It is recommended to give authorizations to a group of users instead of users. When creating a new user, if he is integrated in an existing group, he will automatically inherit the same authorizations than others users of this group for each existing projects / folders /documents with securities.

entries.name: SystemSecurityCommand
entries.title: This command allows you to manage **TopSolid**
entries.summary: It is strongly recommended to use the security by using groups of users instead of directly users. Indeed, in case of adding a new user, he could be added in an existing group and will automatically inherit all these group rights.

entries.name: LifeCycleSubStatesCommand
